title: Long run self-adjustment in the AD-AS model
description: Practice what you've learned about the long-run adjustment of output, employment, and prices to changes in aggregate demand and short-run aggregate supply in this exercise.
topic_path: Long run self-adjustment in the AD-AS model

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following best describes what will happen to this economy in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate grid plots three labeled lines and three points on a graph. The vertical axis is labeled P L, and the horizontal axis is labeled real G D P. One vertical line is labeled L R A S. One upward-sloping line from the bottom left to the top right is labeled S R A S. One downward-sloping line from top left to bottom right is labeled as A D. A point at the intersection between S R A S and A D has two dashed lines extending from it, one horizontally to a labeled point P L subscript 1 on the vertical axis, and one vertically down to a labeled point Y subscript 1 on the horizontal axis. The vertical line L R A S drops to point Y subscript f on the horizontal axis.

choices.content: Wages and prices will increase and output will decrease from $Y_1$ to $Y_f$
Wages and prices will decrease and the full employment level of output will increase
Wages and prices will increase and output will remain at $Y_1$
Wages and prices will decrease and output will decrease from $Y_1$ to $Y_f$
The LRAS curve will shift out and full employment output will increase from $Y_f$ to $Y_1$

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

The graph shown here illustrates the impact of a positive aggregate demand shock on the economy of Johnsrudia.

**If no policy actions are taken by the government of Johnrudia, what will happen to wages and output in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate plane plots four labeled straight lines: A D, S R A S, L R A S subscript 1, and L R A S subscript 2. The horizontal axis is labeled real G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Lines L R A S subscript 1 and L R A S subscript 2 are vertical, with L R A S subscript 2 to the right of L R A S subscript 1. Line A D is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Line S R A S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. The sloping lines intersect at L R A S subscript 1. A rightward arrow is placed between the two vertical lines, indicating a shift of L R A S from L R A S subscript 1 to L R A S subscript 2.

choices.content: Wages will increase; output will decrease
Wages will increase; output will stay at $Y_1$
Wages will not change; output will increase
Wages will decrease; output will decrease
Wages will decrease; output will increase

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume the country of Libertyville is currently producing at its full employment output. 

**What will be the long-run impact of a negative aggregate demand shock on nominal wages and the level of unemployment in Libertyville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Wages decrease; no change in unemployment
Wages decrease; unemployment increases
No change in wages; no change in unemployment
Wages increase; unemployment increases
Wages decrease, unemployment decreases

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume an economy is currently in long-run equilibrium.

**What would be the long-run impact of an increase in consumer confidence?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in the price level
A decrease in unemployment
Economic growth
A decrease in inflation
An increase in long-run aggregate supply (LRAS)

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Maxistan experienced a positive aggregate demand (AD) shock due to unbridled consumer optimism.

**What will be the effect of this AD shock on Maxistan’s level of output and unemployment rate in the long run?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Output will be unaffected; unemployment rate will be unaffected.
Output will increase; unemployment rate will be unaffected.
Output will be unaffected; unemployment will decrease.
Output will increase; unemployment will increase.
Output will increase; unemployment will decrease.

assessment_items.question_content:
The economy of Hamsterville is in long-run equilibrium. The central bank of Hamsterville decided to engage in expansionary monetary policy and to buy bonds. This action increased aggregate demand (AD).

**What is the long-run effect of an increase in AD on the price level and the level of output?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Price level increases; output doesn’t change.
Price level increases; output increases.
Price level decreases; output increases.
Price level decreases; output doesn’t change.

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume that an economy is initially in long-run equilibrium. The economy can return to long-run equilibrium output after a negative shock to aggregate demand (AD).

**Which of the following best describes how this self-correction process will occur?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Lower output and higher unemployment leads to lower wages, causing short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) to increase.
Lower output and higher unemployment leads to lower wages, causing aggregate demand (AD) to decrease.
Higher output and lower unemployment leads to higher wages, causing SRAS to decrease.
Lower output and lower unemployment leads to lower wages, causing long-run aggregate supply to decrease.
Higher output and lower unemployment leads to higher wages, causing short-run aggregate supply to increase.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following graphs correctly shows an economy experiencing economic growth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A coordinate plane plots four labeled straight lines: A D, S R A S, L R A S subscript 1, and L R A S subscript 2. The horizontal axis is labeled real G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Lines L R A S subscript 1 and L R A S subscript 2 are vertical, with L R A S subscript 2 to the right of L R A S subscript 1. Line A D is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Line S R A S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Lines S R A S and A D intersect at line L R A S subscript 1. A rightward arrow is placed between the two vertical lines, indicating a shift in L R A S from L R A S subscript 1 to L R A S subscript 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f169be04492c01de78c752768eff30de36dd49b7)

![A coordinate plane plots four labeled straight lines: A D subscript 1, A D subscript 2, S R A S, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled real G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Line L R A S is vertical and intersects the horizontal axis near the middle. Lines A D subscript 1 and A D subscript 2 are downward-sloping from top left to bottom right, with A D subscript 2 to the right of A D subscript 1. Line S R A S is upward-sloping from bottom left to top right. Lines S R A S and A D subscript 1 intersect at L R A S. A rightward arrow is placed between the two A D curves, indicating a shift in A D from A D subscript 1 to A D subscript 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7734eb33cb96fe918fffd6d0882dccfc97f2a747)

![A coordinate plane plots four labeled straight lines: A D subscript 1, S R A S subscript 1, S R A S subscript 2, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled real G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Line L R A S is vertical and intersects the horizontal axis near the middle. Line A D subscript 1 is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Lines S R A S subscript 1 and S R A S subscript 2 are upward-sloping from bottom left to top right, with S R A S subscript 2 to the right of S R A S subscript 1. Line A D subscript 1 intersects S R A S subscript 1 at L R A S. A rightward arrow is placed between the two S R A S curves, indicating a shift in S R A S from S R A S subscript 1 to S R A S subscript 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9faf2ef6e5bbf35d7603ae3f717f13bcc2f22a66)

![A coordinate plane plots five labeled straight lines: A D subscript 1, A D subscript 2, S R A S subscript 1, S R A S subscript 2, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled real G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Line L R A S is vertical and intersects the horizontal axis near the middle. Lines A D subscript 1 and A D subscript 2 are downward-sloping from top left to bottom right, with A D subscript 2 to the right of A D subscript 1. Lines S R A S subscript 1 and S R A S subscript 2 are upward-sloping from bottom left to top right, with S R A S subscript 2 to the right of S R A S subscript 1. Two rightward arrows are placed—one between the A D curves and one between the S R A S curves—indicating shifts in both A D and S R A S.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1931c11d82874af7bd02d9a8b35646e65c3cdb33)

![A coordinate plane plots five labeled straight lines: A D subscript 1, A D subscript 2, S R A S subscript 1, S R A S subscript 2, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled real G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The axes are unmarked. Line L R A S is vertical and intersects the horizontal axis near the middle. Lines A D subscript 1 and A D subscript 2 are downward-sloping from top left to bottom right, with A D subscript 2 to the right of A D subscript 1. Lines S R A S subscript 1 and S R A S subscript 2 are upward-sloping from bottom left to top right, with S R A S subscript 2 above and to the left of S R A S subscript 1. A rightward arrow is placed between the A D curves, and a leftward arrow is placed between the S R A S curves, indicating opposite shifts in A D and S R A S.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/641a36516326cba0e7dec8eb99944ef5ac7226ad)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes how an outward shift of a country's PPC would be illustrated in the AD-AS model?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve increases
The aggregate demand (AD) curve increases
The short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve increases
A movement to the right along the short-run aggregate supply curve (SRAS)
A movement to the right along the aggregate demand (AD) curve

assessment_items.question_content:
**Full-employment output has definitely increased in which graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A coordinate grid plots six labeled straight lines on a graph. The vertical axis is labeled P L, and the horizontal axis is labeled real G D P. Two vertical lines are labeled L R A S subscript 1 and L R A S subscript 2, with L R A S subscript 2 to the right of L R A S subscript 1. Two upward-sloping lines from the bottom left to the top right are labeled S R A S subscript 1 and S R A S subscript 2, with S R A S subscript 2 above S R A S subscript 1. Two downward-sloping lines are labeled A D subscript 1 and A D subscript 2, with A D subscript 2 to the right of A D subscript 1. The vertical line L R A S subscript 1 drops to point Y subscript f 1 on the horizontal axis. The vertical line L R A S subscript 2 drops to point Y subscript f 2 on the horizontal axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8529e400004a85a2e8539d059629f1df2a2eaddb)

![A coordinate grid plots three labeled straight lines. The vertical axis is labeled P L, and the horizontal axis is labeled real G D P. An upward-sloping line from the bottom left to the top right is labeled S R A S. Two downward-sloping lines from the top left to the bottom right are labeled A D 1 and A D 2, with A D 2 to the right of A D 1. The line A D 1 intersects S R A S at a point corresponding to P L 1 on the vertical axis and Y 1 on the horizontal axis. The line A D 2 intersects S R A S at a higher point labeled P L 2, aligned above Y 2 on the horizontal axis. Horizontal dashed lines extend from P L 1 and P L 2 to the vertical axis. Vertical dashed lines extend from Y 1 and Y 2 to the horizontal axis.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e70123d0b04f3156ee5cae3f65eddee46d924d35)

![A coordinate plane plots four labeled straight lines: A D, S R A S subscript 1, S R A S subscript 2, and L R A S. The horizontal axis is labeled real G D P, and the vertical axis is labeled P subscript L. The axes are unmarked. Line A D is downward-sloping from top left to bottom right. Lines S R A S subscript 1 and S R A S subscript 2 are upward-sloping from bottom left to top right, with S R A S subscript 1 above S R A S subscript 2. Line L R A S is vertical. S R A S subscript 1 intersects A D at the point where a dashed vertical line drops to Y subscript 1 on the horizontal axis and a dashed horizontal line extends to P subscript L subscript 1 on the vertical axis. S R A S subscript 2 intersects A D at the point where a dashed vertical line drops to Y subscript 2, which is also labeled Y subscript f. A dashed horizontal line extends from this point to the vertical axis and is labeled P subscript L subscript 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4e35d4330ddd389c434837c04cf221d1653c492f)

![A coordinate grid plots three labeled lines and two points on a graph. The vertical axis is labeled nominal interest rate, and the horizontal axis is labeled Q of dollar. One downward-sloping line from top left to bottom right is labeled D subscript M. Two vertical lines are labeled S subscript M and S subscript M 2, with S subscript M 2 to the right of S subscript M. Two points mark the intersections of D subscript M with S subscript M and S subscript M subscript 2. A horizontal dashed line extends from i subscript 1 on the vertical axis to the left intersection. Another horizontal dashed line extends from i subscript 2 to the right intersection.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7fcab26c47e53953151258ad7c7868ab190b24be)

![A coordinate grid plots five labeled lines and five labeled points on a graph. The vertical axis is labeled P L, and the horizontal axis is labeled real G D P. One vertical line is labeled L R A S. The vertical line drops to point Y subscript 1, Y subscript f on the horizontal axis. One downward-sloping line from the top left to the bottom right is labeled A D. Two upward-sloping lines from the bottom left to the top right are labeled S R A S subscript 1 and S R A S subscript 2, with S R A S subscript 2 above S R A S subscript 1. Point Y subscript 2 is labeled on the horizontal axis, to the left of point Y subscript 1. The A D line intersects the S R A S subscript 1 line and the L R A S line at a point where two dashed lines extend, one horizontally to P L subscript 1 on the vertical axis and one vertically to Y subscript 1 on the horizontal axis. The A D line intersects the S R A S subscript 2 line at a point where two dashed lines extend, one horizontally to P L subscript 2 and one vertically to Y subscript 2.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/23e6bd8b23b1dd2f18d3bf90d3b5ae3286254447)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios illustrates an increase in the full-employment level of output?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve shifts to the right.
The aggregate demand (AD) curve shifts to the right.
The short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve shifts to the right.
The money supply shifts to the right.
The demand for money shifts to the right.

assessment_items.question_content:
**How are an increased potential output and an unchanged current output represented in the aggregate demand-aggregate supply (AD-AS) model?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve shifts to the right.
The aggregate demand (AD) curve shifts to the right.
The short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve shifts to the right.
The money supply shifts to the right.
The demand for money shifts to the right.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the best description of economic growth in the AD-AS model?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A shift of the long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve to the right
A shift of the long-run aggregate supply (LRAS) curve to the left
A shift to the right of the aggregate demand (AD) curve
A shift to the right of the short-run aggregate supply (SRAS) curve
A leftward shift of the aggregate demand (AD) curve

assessment_items.question_content:
The current state of the economy of Fredland is illustrated in this graph:

![The figure presents a graph of 3 lines in the first quadrant of a coordinate plane. The horizontal axis is labeled “real G D P” and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The first line is labeled A D and begins slightly to the right of the vertical axis and above the horizontal axis. It moves downward and to the right and ends to the right of horizontal axis value Y F just above the horizontal axis. The second line is labeled S R A S and begins very slightly to the right of the vertical axis, and slightly above the horizontal axis. It moves upward and to the right, intersecting with line A D to the left of point Y F. It ends far to the right of the vertical axis and far above the horizontal axis. The third line is vertical and is labeled L R A S. It begins at the point Y F on the horizontal axis and extends upward, first crossing the line A D and then crossing the line S R A S. It then ends at a point near the top of the coordinate plane. 
](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/60c0817ae48f435a27f11a35d876352192ce21ea)

**Which of the following best describes how the economy will adjust in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Short-run aggregate supply will decrease and the price level will increase.
Long-run aggregate supply will decrease and the price level will not change.
Aggregate demand will decrease and the price level will increase.
Aggregate demand will increase and the price level will increase.
Long-run aggregate supply will increase and the price level will stay the same.

assessment_items.question_content:
The current state of the economy of Fredland is illustrated in this graph:

![The figure presents a graph of 3 lines in the first quadrant of a coordinate plane. The horizontal axis is labeled “real G D P” and the vertical axis is labeled P L. The first line is labeled A D and begins slightly to the right of the vertical axis and above the horizontal axis. It moves downward and to the right and ends to the right of horizontal axis value Y F just above the horizontal axis. The second line is labeled S R A S and begins very slightly to the right of the vertical axis, and slightly above the horizontal axis. It moves upward and to the right, intersecting with line A D to the left of point Y F. It ends far to the right of the vertical axis and far above the horizontal axis. The third line is vertical and is labeled L R A S. It begins at the point Y F on the horizontal axis and extends upward, first crossing the line A D and then crossing the line S R A S. It then ends at a point near the top of the coordinate plane.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/60c0817ae48f435a27f11a35d876352192ce21ea)

The government is considering whether to allow the economy to self-adjust or take fiscal policy actions to correct this graph.

**In the long run, how does the change in unemployment and the price level compare if the government takes an appropriate fiscal policy action rather than allowing the economy to self-adjust?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
If the economy is allowed to self-adjust in the long run, short-run aggregate supply decreases and the price level increases.
If the government takes an appropriate action, it will pursue contractionary fiscal policy, such as decreasing government spending or increasing taxes.
Contractionary fiscal policy decreases aggregate demand and the price level.
In the long run, unemployment is higher and the price level is higher if the economy self-adjusts, and unemployment is higher and the price level is lower if contractionary fiscal policy is implemented.

choices.content: Unemployment increases in both approaches, but the price level will be lower if the government takes action rather than allowing the economy to self adjust.
Unemployment decreases in both approaches, and the price level will be higher if the government takes action rather than allowing the economy to self adjust.
Unemployment increases in both cases and the price level increases in both cases.
Unemployment increases in both approaches, but the price level is unaffected if the government intervenes instead of allowing the economy to self adjust.
Unemployment decreases in both approaches, but the price level will be higher if the government takes action rather than allowing the economy to self adjust.

assessment_items.question_content:
The economies of Fredland and Hamsterville are trading partners and Fredland’s current account is balanced.

**Which of the following best describes what happens if national income in Fredland increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The current account will move to a deficit.
The current account will move to a surplus.
The current account is unaffected.
Imports by Fredland from Hamsterville decrease.
Exports from Hamsterville to Fredland decrease.
